steps.title: Preserve the paired phrase
steps.content: The sentence uses the paired construction not only and but also to connect two parallel prepositional phrases: by cyclones and by vapor streams. Because those two phrases are part of one correlative structure, no punctuation should interrupt the connection between them.

steps.title: Remove internal breaks
steps.content: Choice A places a dash after cyclones, breaking the link before but also. Choice B surrounds not only by cyclones with dashes, treating it as an interruption instead of part of the core structure. Choice C uses commas in the same disruptive way. The remaining choice keeps caused not only by cyclones but also by vapor streams as one smooth, parallel construction. The answer is D.
